title: Spectrophotometric Determination of Vanadium with N-Benzoyl-N- p-chlorophenylhydroxyIanine
author: A. K. Majumdar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A highly sensitive spcetrophothmetrie method for determinaticn of traces of vanadium in presence of all ions, except tungstato and molybdato, by  N -benzoyl- N - \(p\) -chlorophenylhydroxylamine has been proposed. The violet complex on extraction into chloroform obeys Beer's law at its maximum absorption region (530 m µ ) with the optimum range from 2-8  µ g. whore the % relative error is only 2.7. The complex, with the dissociation constant in the order of 10 -8 . contains the metal and the reagent in a ratio of 1:2.
steps_raw: A highly sensitive spcetrophothmetrie method for determinaticn of traces of vanadium in presence of all ions, except tungstato and molybdato, by  N -benzoyl- N - \(p\) -chlorophenylhydroxylamine has been proposed. The violet complex on extraction into chloroform obeys Beer's law at its maximum absorption region (530 m µ ) with the optimum range from 2-8  µ g. whore the % relative error is only 2.7. The complex, with the dissociation constant in the order of 10 -8 . contains the metal and the reagent in a ratio of 1:2.
